nativeRange.habitat: Coastal marine and brackish environments from the intertidal zone down to shallow subtidal depths. The species grows both attached in tufts on hard substrate and as free-floating unattached masses.
nativeRange.biotope: Shallow coastal marine, estuarine, and brackish photic zone

decorPreferences: open water or loose on rubble

avoidWith: aggressive herbivorous fish such as rabbitfish or surgeonfish that may graze it heavily

imageCandidates.notes: Product photo from AlgaeBarn Clean Chaeto listing; shows dense green filamentous clump characteristic of the species.

summary: Clean Chaeto is widely regarded as the most popular macroalga for reef refugiums, valued for its fast growth, reliable nutrient uptake, and the dense clump structure that shelters copepods and amphipods before they seed the main display.
careNotes:
Clean Chaeto is the aquarium trade name for cultured, parasite-free stock of Chaetomorpha linum, a cosmopolitan green macroalga in the family Cladophoraceae. The species occurs naturally in marine, brackish, and occasionally freshwater coastal habitats across the Atlantic, Pacific, and Mediterranean.

The alga forms dense, intertwined clumps of wiry, unbranched filaments that resemble a green scouring pad. This tangled structure is valuable not only for nutrient uptake but also as habitat for copepods, amphipods, and isopods — the interior spaces provide refuge for beneficial microfauna that can migrate from the refugium into the main display tank.

Chaeto is generally considered among the easiest macroalgae to keep. It tends to thrive under medium-to-high full-spectrum lighting and steady water flow. The most common and effective setup is to allow it to tumble freely in a refugium sump, where flow keeps the mass turning so all sides receive light. Under adequate lighting, growth is reported to be fast — doubling in size within weeks is a figure commonly cited by hobbyists. Regularly harvesting and discarding excess growth is the primary mechanism by which the alga exports nutrients from the system.

Standard reef water parameters are recommended: salinity around SG 1.023–1.026, temperature 22–27°C, pH 8.1–8.4, and alkalinity 8–12 dKH. No supplemental CO2 or specialized fertilization is typically required; the alga draws nutrients directly from the water column.

The species has also been noted to tolerate somewhat lower salinity, consistent with its natural distribution in estuarine environments. There is ongoing taxonomic debate over whether Chaetomorpha linum and Chaetomorpha aerea represent the same taxon, though this distinction has no practical bearing on aquarium care.
breedingNotes: Propagation in captivity is achieved almost exclusively through fragmentation — detached filament masses readily continue growing when placed in suitable conditions. Sexual reproduction (alternation of gametophyte and sporophyte generations involving biflagellate gametes and zoospores) occurs in the wild but has not been reported as a meaningful propagation pathway in hobby aquariums.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Chaetomorpha linum
additional.notes: Cross-confirmed taxonomy: family Cladophoraceae, order Cladophorales. Confirmed native range includes British Isles, Europe, Mediterranean, Atlantic North America, and California. Noted unbranched uniseriate filament morphology and cosmopolitan distribution. Confirmed brackish and freshwater tolerance. Documents taxonomic debate with C. aerea.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Chaetomorpha
additional.notes: Confirmed genus is cosmopolitan marine/brackish; confirmed filamentous unbranched uniseriate morphology and fragmentation as asexual propagation vector.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros
additional.notes: Independently confirms fast growth rate, beginner care level, refugium tumbling as primary use, and nutrient-absorbing capacity. Also notes brackish water compatibility.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
